about.heading: An organisation that has been working for over 40 years
about.p1: The Student Government of Wrocław University of Economics and Business is an organisation that for over 40 years has represented students’ interests before the university authorities, the city and national institutions. We run projects, organise events and make sure the student voice is heard.
about.p2: We bring together people who care. Anyone of you can join our team and help shape the UEW academic community.
about.quote: We don’t wait for change. We create it.

adaptacja.name: Adaptation
adaptacja.description: A welcome programme for first-years. We help you find your feet at the university from day one.

juwenalia.name: Economic Juwenalia
juwenalia.description: The biggest UEW student festival. Three days of music, sport and fun.

tarcza.name: Student Shield
tarcza.description: Free legal help for students. We advise on regulatory matters and beyond.

p1.heading: We build a student community
p1.lead: University is the people you meet.
p1.body: From Adapciak and Adaptation Days to the UEW Ball, TEDxUEW and UE Party — all year round we create events that bring students together, help them grow and stay in memory for years.

p2.heading: We’re on your side
p2.lead: When you need help, we’re right here.
p2.body: Student rights, scholarships, psychological support, the Student Ombudsman — in the Student zone we’ve gathered everything you need in your everyday life at the university.

partnerzy.metaDesc: Join the partners of the UEW Student Government. Reach 11,000 students, be present at the biggest events and support young talent.
partnerzy.heroLead: We connect your brand with UEW’s largest student community. See how we can work together.
partnerzy.benefitsHeading: Why partner with the Student Government

reach.title: Reach 11,000 students
reach.desc: A direct channel to UEW’s largest student community.

events.title: Presence at events
events.desc: Adapciak, the UEW Ball, TEDxUEW, UE Party — your brand where the students are.

image.title: A student-friendly brand image
image.desc: Working with the Student Government is real support for the academic community.

talent.title: Access to young talent
talent.desc: Recruitment, internships and ambassador programmes among future economists.

partnerzy.wallSub: Click an open slot to see what your presence could look like.

partnerzy.ctaAria: Go to the Work with us page

start.q: How do we start working with the Student Government?
start.a: Write to the person responsible for external relations — we’ll prepare an offer tailored to your goals. Contact: Karol Vogel, karol.vogel@samorzad.ue.wroc.pl, tel. +48 511 599 376.

forms.q: What forms of cooperation do you offer?
forms.a: Event organisation and partner zones, social media promotion (TikTok, Instagram, Facebook) and marketing campaigns tailored to the UEW student.

reach.q: How many students do you reach?
reach.a: We represent over 11,000 students of Wrocław University of Economics and Business.

tailored.q: Will you prepare an offer for a specific goal?
tailored.a: Yes — we tailor the offer to your product or service and to your campaign goals.

wspolpracuj.metaDesc: Reach 11,000 UEW students with your brand — events, social media promotion and marketing campaigns. Get in touch with our external relations board member.
wspolpracuj.heroLead: Let’s connect your brand with UEW’s largest student community — choose a form of cooperation and we’ll tailor the offer to you.
wspolpracuj.offerHeading: Want to reach students with your brand?
wspolpracuj.offerLead: We’ll help promote your product or service among UEW students. We’ll prepare an offer tailored to your goals — pick a form of cooperation and we’ll handle the rest.

events.title: Event organisation
events.desc: Joint events, partner zones and a presence where the students are.

social.title: Social media promotion
social.desc: Reaching the student community on TikTok, Instagram and Facebook.

campaigns.title: Marketing campaigns
campaigns.desc: Activities tailored to your product or service and to the UEW student.

kontakt.metaDesc: Get in touch with the Student Government of Wrocław University of Economics and Business — form, email, address (ul. Kamienna 43) and social media.
kontakt.heroLead: We’re here for you. Choose the way that suits you best — the form, email or social media.
kontakt.detailsLead: Got a question, an idea for cooperation or something to sort out? Write to us — we reply as fast as we can.
kontakt.errEmail: Enter a valid email address, e.g. jan@example.com.
kontakt.errMessage: Your message is too short (min. 10 characters).
kontakt.successBody: Your message has been received. We’ll get back to you as soon as possible.
kontakt.mapTitle: Map — SSUEW Student Government office, ul. Kamienna 43, Wrocław

naszaDzialalnosc.metaDesc: We act for students, support them in the fight for student rights and inspire new initiatives. Get to know the mission of the UEW Student Government.
naszaDzialalnosc.heroLead: We act for students, support you in the fight for student rights and inspire you to take up new initiatives.
naszaDzialalnosc.missionHeading: We act, we support, we inspire

act.title: We act
act.desc: For students — we run projects, organise events and represent you before the university, the city and other institutions.

support.title: We support
support.desc: We stand by your side in the fight for student rights and care for the wellbeing of every student at our University.

inspire.title: We inspire
inspire.desc: We encourage new initiatives, pursuing your passions, self-development and shaping civic attitudes.

naszaDzialalnosc.quote: “The Student Government’s overriding goal is the constant support and wellbeing of every student at our University.”
naszaDzialalnosc.ctaHeading: See what we create all year round
naszaDzialalnosc.ctaDesc: From Adapciak to TEDxUEW — discover the projects students co-create.
naszaDzialalnosc.ctaAria: Go to the Our projects page

naszeProjekty.metaDesc: Adapciak, Animalia, the UEW Ball, Graduetion, Economic Bridges, TEDxUEW and more — projects through which we bring UEW students together and help them grow.
naszeProjekty.heroLead: Because studying is more than lectures. We run projects that bring students together, support their growth and enrich academic life at UEW.
naszeProjekty.heading: Every year we create these events

adapciak.desc: An eight-day camp for first-years, full of attractions and themed parties — bonding on the final stretch before the year begins.

animalia.desc: A charity project for endangered species — two events (at Wrocław ZOO and a beach bar) in partnership with the DODO Foundation.

bal.desc: A joint initiative of the University authorities and the Student Government. At the ball we present the “Ekonomka” statuettes to distinguished staff.

dni.desc: Talks and training that introduce first-years to academic life — rights, duties and opportunities to grow.

graduetion.desc: An American-style diploma ceremony — gowns, caps and the traditional cap toss for the finale.

mosty.desc: A four-day exchange between students of five partner economics universities: lectures, workshops and case studies.

test.desc: An inter-university quiz modelled on TVP’s Great Test — heats at each university and a grand final of the best.

tedx.desc: A local TEDx — ideas worth spreading in a few minutes of inspiring talks from speakers.

party.desc: A series of student parties for all of student Wrocław — a moment of relaxation and fun beyond your duties.

struktura.metaDesc: How the UEW Student Government is built — the Chair, the Board, the University Council, the Audit Committee and the Student Electoral Committee.
struktura.heroLead: Get to know the Student Government’s bodies and who is responsible for what — from strategy to overseeing elections.
struktura.heading: How the Student Government is built

top.title: Chair & Vice-chairs
top.role: They shape the Student Government’s development strategy and make the most important decisions.

board.title: The Board
board.role: The executive body — delivers the goals set out in the Higher Education Act (Ustawa 2.0).

russ.title: University Council of the Student Government
russ.role: The legislative body — sets internal regulations and reviews higher-level legal acts.

audit.title: Audit Committee
audit.role: The oversight body — supervises the activities and finances of the Student Government.

election.title: Student Electoral Committee
election.role: The electoral body — prepares, runs and oversees elections to the Student Government.

russ.metaTitle: University Council of the Student Government
russ.metaDesc: RUSS — the legislative and advisory body of the UEW Student Government. 15 councillors represent students’ demands. See the Council’s powers and members.
russ.heroTitle: University Council of the Student Government
russ.heroLead: The legislative voice of students — RUSS reviews, decides and watches over the Student Government’s work.
russ.introHeading: The student voice in one place
russ.introBody: RUSS is the legislative and advisory body of the Student Government. 15 councillors, elected in general elections for a one-year term, represent students’ demands during sessions and votes.
competencies: Reviewing matters related to teaching and scholarship benefits
Taking positions on matters concerning the university’s students
Reviewing the work of units and bodies dealing with student affairs
Electing the Chair, approving the Board and granting discharge
Overseeing the work of the Student Government’s bodies
russ.footnote: The composition may change during the term.

wladze.metaDesc: The Rector and Vice-Rectors of Wrocław University of Economics and Business — who leads the University and oversees student affairs, education, research and finance.
wladze.heroLead: The Rector and Vice-Rectors of Wrocław University of Economics and Business — the people who lead the University and oversee its most important areas.
wladze.heading: Who leads the University
wladze.rektorRole: His Magnificence the Rector of Wrocław University of Economics and Business

roles.student: Vice-Rector for Student Affairs and Education

dziekani.metaDesc: The dean’s office for student affairs at Wrocław University of Economics and Business — the dean and vice-deans assigned to individual fields of study.
dziekani.heroLead: The dean’s office for student affairs. Matters such as an individual study plan, leave or appeals are handled by the vice-dean assigned to your field of study.
dziekani.heading: Find the vice-dean for your field of study
dziekani.intro: Student matters — from an individual study plan to appeals — are handled by vice-deans assigned to particular fields of study. Check who is responsible for yours.

przewodniczacy.metaDesc: The leadership of the UEW Student Government — the Chair and Vice-chairs, their areas of responsibility and contact details.
przewodniczacy.heroLead: The leadership of the Student Government — the people who shape strategy, make the key decisions and represent students before the University.
przewodniczacy.heading: The people who lead the Student Government
przewodniczacy.chairDesc: The statutory representative of all the University’s students, working for the student community through the Government’s governing, executive and legislative bodies.
przewodniczacy.wiceIntro: The Vice-chairs support the Chair in the Student Government’s day-to-day work, delivering tasks in their assigned areas and coordinating selected activities and projects.

skw.metaDesc: The Student Electoral Committee (SKW) of the UEW Student Government — duties, members and resolutions. The body that prepares and oversees the government elections.
skw.heroLead: A three-person body that prepares, runs and oversees elections to the Student Government’s bodies — so that they are fair and transparent.
skw.heading: Student Electoral Committee
skw.intro: The Student Electoral Committee (SKW) is a three-person body of the Student Government that prepares, runs and oversees elections to the governing bodies of Wrocław University of Economics and Business.
zadania: Setting the detailed electoral calendar and making it public — no later than 14 days before the elections.
Preparing the lists of voters entitled to vote.
Organising electoral meetings and running them smoothly.
Ensuring the electoral procedures run correctly.

rzecznik.metaDesc: The UEW Student Ombudsman — Jakub Buchta — helps students interpret regulations and represents them before the university. See how to get help.
rzecznik.heroLead: When the rules are unclear and your case is stuck — the Ombudsman will help you understand them and stand by your side.
rzecznik.quote: “The Student Ombudsman helps students interpret regulations and represents them in dealings with the university’s administrative units.”
rzecznik.introBody: Get in touch if you can’t interpret the rules, couldn’t resolve your case at the dean’s office, or have doubts about your rights as a student.

duties.interpret: Help interpreting the university’s regulations and rules
duties.injustice: Support in cases of injustice when the dean’s office hasn’t resolved the matter
duties.consult: Advice on difficult matters concerning students’ rights and duties
duties.direct: Pointing you to the right sources of information at the university and in the Student Government

check.title: Check the Infopacks and the Study Regulations
check.desc: Many answers are at hand — start with the materials in the Student zone.

describe.title: Describe your case
describe.desc: Gather the facts: what happened, who you spoke to and which regulations the case concerns.

write.title: Write to the Ombudsman
write.desc: Send a message to rps@samorzad.ue.wroc.pl or use the contact form.

together.title: We act together
together.desc: The Ombudsman will help interpret the rules and — if needed — represent you before the university.

who.a: Someone who helps students interpret regulations and represents them in dealings with the university’s administrative units. The role is held by Jakub Buchta.

when.a: When you can’t interpret the rules, couldn’t resolve your case at the dean’s office, or have doubts about your rights and duties as a student.

how.q: How do I get in touch?
how.a: Write to rps@samorzad.ue.wroc.pl or use the contact form. First gather the facts: what happened, who you spoke to and which regulations the case concerns.

what.q: What can the Ombudsman do for me?
what.a: Help interpret the rules, point you to the right sources of information and, if needed, represent you before the university.

prawaStudenta.metaDesc: Get to know your rights at Wrocław University of Economics and Business — from an individual study plan to a board exam. The Higher Education Act in plain language.
prawaStudenta.heroLead: Studying isn’t only duties. Get to know the rights you have at every stage — and where to find support when they’re broken.
prawaStudenta.heading: Every student has the right to know their rights
prawaStudenta.intro: Under the Higher Education Act, every student has the right to training on their rights and duties. The student government organises this training together with the Students’ Parliament of the Republic of Poland.

repeat.title: Repeating classes
repeat.desc: The right to repeat certain classes in the event of unsatisfactory academic results, on the terms set out in the university regulations.
repeat.expl: If you fail a subject, you have the right to repeat it under the study regulations — you’re not automatically expelled.

ios.title: Individual study plan
ios.desc: The option to apply for an individual study plan — flexibly adapting the curriculum to your needs.
ios.expl: Do you work, train competitively or have another important life situation? You can apply for a flexible plan and individual assessment dates.

absence.title: Excused absence
absence.desc: The right to excuse an absence and take leave from classes, including sitting exams at another date.
absence.expl: Illness or an important life event? You have the right to excuse an absence and sit the exam at another date.

change.title: Changing your field of study
change.desc: The Act allows you to change your field of study while following set procedures.
change.expl: Feel it’s not the right field? You can change it, and subjects you’ve already passed will be recognised under the procedure.

ects.title: Transferring and recognising ECTS credits
ects.desc: The right to transfer and recognise ECTS credits from other universities — domestic and foreign — in line with mobility rules.
ects.expl: Credits earned at another university, including abroad (e.g. Erasmus), can be recognised — you don’t pass the same thing twice.

commission.title: Board exam
commission.desc: The right to take a board exam, with an observer of your choice present.
commission.expl: Disagree with an exam grade? You can request a board exam, and an observer you choose has the right to be present.

mode.title: Changing your study mode
mode.desc: The option to transfer between full-time and part-time studies, in line with the university regulations.
mode.expl: Has your situation changed (e.g. work)? You can move from full-time to part-time studies or vice versa, under the regulations.

source.q: Where do my rights as a student come from?
source.a: From the Higher Education Act (Ustawa 2.0) and the UEW study regulations. The Act guarantees, among other things, the right to training on students’ rights and duties.

ios.q: What is an individual study plan (IOS)?
ios.a: It’s the option to flexibly adapt the curriculum and organisation of your studies to your situation. You apply for it on the terms set out in the university regulations.

commission.q: Can I take a board exam?
commission.a: Yes. You have the right to a board exam, with an observer of your choice present.

report.q: Where do I report that my rights are being broken?
report.a: Contact the Student Ombudsman — they’ll help interpret the rules and, if needed, represent you before the university’s units.

prawo.metaDesc: The most important UEW legal acts in one place — the Act, the statute, the study regulations, orders and university regulations.
prawo.heroLead: Regulations can be complex — we’ve gathered the most important legal acts in force at UEW in one place.
prawo.intro: Here you’ll find the key regulations that govern our University — from acts of law to internal university regulations.

act.title: The Law on Higher Education and Science

statute.title: Statute of Wrocław University of Economics and Business

regulacje.metaDesc: The key documents that govern the UEW Student Government — regulations, the electoral rules and the visual identity.
regulacje.heroLead: Clearly and in one place — the documents that govern how the Student Government works.
regulacje.intro: Below you’ll find the key internal regulations that govern the UEW Student Government.

documents.ros: Regulations of the Council of Student Organisations

zarzadzenia.metaDesc: Orders of the Chair of the UEW Student Government — executive acts from the current term and access to archived documents.
zarzadzenia.heroLead: Executive acts that govern the day-to-day work of the Student Government. Here you’ll find the full set of orders from the current term.
zarzadzenia.heading: Chair’s orders
zarzadzenia.intro: The Chair’s orders are executive acts that organise the day-to-day work of the Student Government. We share documents from the current term in a single shared folder.
zarzadzenia.termCardDesc: The full set of current orders in a Google Drive folder.
zarzadzenia.archiveDesc: We share archived orders from previous terms on request — write to us and we’ll send you the document you need.

infopacki.metaDesc: Practical guides for UEW students: USOS, the Study Regulations, passing the semester, applications, the library, graduation and more.
infopacki.heroLead: Short, practical guides to the most important student matters — from USOS to the graduation process.
infopacki.intro: Infopacks are short, practical guides that help you understand regulations and applications and move easily around our university’s online spaces.
infopacki.notFoundText: Didn’t find the answer to your question?

packs.usos: How do I navigate the USOS system?
packs.studyReg: What are the key rules in the University Study Regulations?
packs.semester: What are the rules for passing a semester?
packs.deanInfo: What information is worth knowing from the dean’s office?
packs.applications: How, where and when do I submit university applications?
packs.library: How do I use the library and the library system?
packs.life: How do I get involved in student life beyond classes?
packs.diploma: How does the graduation process work?

mapa.metaDesc: Find the key points on the campus of Wrocław University of Economics and Business — the Student Government office, faculties and library. Download the map and get directions.
mapa.heroLead: Don’t get lost on day one. See where to find us and how to get around the UEW campus.
mapa.heading: Key points

office.name: Student Government office
office.note: This is where to find us — drop by with a question or an idea.

campus.name: Main UEW campus
campus.note: Most faculties, the dean’s offices and the Main Library.

mapa.mapTitle: Campus map of Wrocław University of Economics and Business

pomoc.metaDesc: Need support? We’ve gathered the forms of psychological help available at UEW and in Wrocław, plus helplines. You don’t have to cope alone.
pomoc.heroLead: Your wellbeing matters. We’ve gathered trusted forms of support in one place — at the university and beyond.
pomoc.reassureHeading: If you’re having a hard time right now, you don’t have to face it alone.
pomoc.reassureBody: Don’t be afraid to ask for support. We’ve prepared an infopack with the key information about the forms of help available — at the university and beyond.

contains.places: Places at the university that offer help
contains.institutions: Institutions and support points in Wrocław
contains.contacts: Contacts to organisations — phones, chats and other forms

helplines.adults: Helpline for adults in emotional crisis
helplines.youth: Helpline for children and young people
helplines.crisis: Support Centre for people in mental crisis — 24/7
helplines.emergency: Emergency number — in a situation of direct threat to life

pomoc.emergencyNote: In a situation of direct threat to life or health, always call the emergency number 112.

print.title: Print the application from USOSweb
print.desc: You generate and print the application from your student account in the USOSweb system.

submit.title: Submit the application with documentation
submit.desc: Submit the signed application together with the required documentation at the Student Service Office.

decision.title: Collect the decision in USOSweb
decision.desc: Payment of the benefit requires collecting the decision in the USOSweb system. Notifications are sent to your university email address.

wsparcie.metaDesc: An overview of all forms of financial support for UEW students — social, merit and disability scholarships, hardship grants and the university’s own fund.
wsparcie.heroLead: Scholarships, hardship grants and funds you can use during your studies. We’ve gathered them in one place to make finding the right path easier.
wsparcie.heading: Every form of support in one place
wsparcie.intro: The University offers several benefits that can help you get through your studies with peace of mind. Some of them can be received at the same time — choose what fits your situation.

socjalne.title: Social scholarship
socjalne.desc: For students in a more difficult financial situation, awarded on the basis of family income.

zapomoga.title: Hardship grant
zapomoga.desc: One-off help in a sudden, temporarily difficult life situation.

niepelnosprawni.title: Disability scholarship
niepelnosprawni.desc: For students with a current disability certificate — independent of income.

rektora.title: Rector’s scholarship
rektora.desc: For the best students — for high academic results and achievements.

fundusz.title: Own Scholarship Fund grant
fundusz.desc: One-off support for good academic results, handled in the LBD system.

wsparcie.ctaDesc: We’ve gathered the procedures, deadlines and practical tips in our infopacks.

stypendia.metaDesc: Social, merit and disability scholarships and hardship grants at Wrocław University of Economics and Business. Documents, deadlines and links.
stypendia.heroLead: Here you’ll find the key information about scholarships and benefits, plus all the documents you need — in one place.
stypendia.intro: During your studies you can use various forms of financial support offered by the University — both benefits for those in a more difficult financial situation and scholarships for very good academic results and scientific, sporting and artistic achievements.

socjalne.title: Social
socjalne.desc: For students in a more difficult financial situation.

rektora.title: Rector’s
rektora.desc: For very good academic results and scientific, sporting or artistic achievements.

niepelnosprawni.title: For people with disabilities
niepelnosprawni.desc: Support for students with a certified disability.

zapomoga.title: Hardship grant
zapomoga.desc: One-off help in a sudden, temporarily difficult life situation.

documents: List of documents for applying for a social scholarship
Rules for calculating income in a student’s family
Rules for assessing academic, artistic and sporting achievements
Data for determining scholarships and grants
Questionnaire for a student with a certified disability
Rules for accommodation in student dormitories
Declaration of no change in financial situation
Declaration of not running a joint household with parents

where.q: Where do I submit a scholarship application?
where.a: You print every benefit application from your student account in the USOSweb system. You submit the signed application together with the required documentation at the Student Service Office.

when.q: When will the scholarship be paid?
when.a: Payment requires collecting the decision in the USOSweb system. Decision notifications are sent to your university email address.

multiple.q: Can I receive several benefits at once?
multiple.a: Yes. For example, the disability scholarship is independent of income and can be combined with other benefits. The details are set out in the scholarship benefits regulations.

firstTime.q: I’m applying for a social scholarship for the first time — what should I remember?
firstTime.a: Attach an income declaration and full documentation confirming your family’s income. If you have an adult sibling who is studying, attach a current certificate of continued education.

diff.q: How does a hardship grant differ from a social scholarship?
diff.a: A hardship grant is one-off help in a sudden, temporarily difficult life event, awarded independently of the social scholarship.

stypRektora.metaDesc: The UEW Rector’s scholarship for very good academic results and scientific, sporting and artistic achievements. How to apply via USOSweb and where to find the regulations.
stypRektora.heroLead: For very good academic results and scientific, sporting or artistic achievements. A reward for the University’s best students.
stypRektora.heading: For those who give a little more
stypRektora.intro: The Rector’s scholarship is awarded to the best students for high academic results and scientific, artistic or sporting achievements. The detailed criteria and the way achievements are scored are set out in the scholarship benefits regulations.

notes.title: What counts
notes.desc: Your grade average and documented scientific, artistic and sporting achievements are taken into account — in line with the assessment rules in the regulations.

notes.title: Own Scholarship Fund grant
notes.desc: In addition to the Rector’s scholarship, UEW awards one-off support from its own fund for good academic results — the application is handled in the LBD system.

stypSocjalne.metaDesc: A social scholarship for UEW students in a more difficult financial situation — how to apply via USOSweb, what documentation to prepare and what to remember.
stypSocjalne.heroLead: For students in a more difficult financial situation. The benefit is awarded on the basis of the student’s family income.
stypSocjalne.heading: Support when the household budget doesn’t add up
stypSocjalne.intro: The social scholarship is available to students in a difficult financial situation. The amount depends on the income per person in the family, calculated in line with the benefits regulations.

notes.title: Applying for the first time?
notes.desc: First-time applicants must submit a printed, signed application together with an income declaration and full documentation confirming the family’s income.

notes.title: Adult sibling who is studying
notes.desc: If you have an adult sibling who is studying, attach a current certificate of continued education to your summer-semester application.

notes.title: Change in family situation
notes.desc: Report changes such as a death, the birth of a child, an adult sibling finishing their education or getting married — they affect the amount of the benefit.

notes.title: Payment after collecting the decision
notes.desc: The scholarship is paid after you collect the decision in the USOSweb system. Notifications are sent to your university email address.

stypNiepelnosprawni.metaDesc: A scholarship for UEW students with a certified disability — how to apply via USOSweb and what certificate to attach.
stypNiepelnosprawni.heroLead: Support for students with a certified disability — independent of financial situation and of other benefits.
stypNiepelnosprawni.heading: A benefit independent of income
stypNiepelnosprawni.intro: The scholarship is available to students who hold a current disability certificate. It does not depend on your financial situation and can be received alongside other benefits.

notes.title: Current certificate
notes.desc: The benefit is awarded on the basis of a valid disability certificate. Once the certificate expires, you must submit new documentation.

notes.title: Payment after collecting the decision
notes.desc: As with other benefits, payment follows after you collect the decision in the USOSweb system. Notifications are sent to your university email address.

zapomoga.metaDesc: A hardship grant is one-off help for UEW students who find themselves in a sudden, temporarily difficult life situation. How to apply via USOSweb.
zapomoga.heroLead: One-off help for students who, for reasons beyond their control, find themselves in a temporarily difficult life situation.
zapomoga.heading: Immediate support in a sudden situation
zapomoga.intro: A hardship grant is a one-off benefit for students who are temporarily in a difficult life situation — for example, due to an unforeseen event. It is awarded independently of the social scholarship.

notes.title: When to apply
notes.desc: You apply for a hardship grant in connection with a specific, temporarily difficult unforeseen event. Attach documents confirming the circumstances to your application.

notes.title: Payment after collecting the decision
notes.desc: Payment requires collecting the decision in the USOSweb system. Notifications are sent to your university email address.
